title: Buttery Creamy Dutch Eggs Bowl
description: A velvety Dutch-inspired bowl featuring poached eggs in a rich buttercream sauce, toasted pine nuts, fresh spinach, smoked salmon, and feta for an energizing start or midday meal.
instructions: Set a medium saucepan of water to a gentle simmer and add a splash of vinegar to help the egg whites coagulate.
Crack each egg into a small bowl, then slide carefully into the simmering water; poach for 3 minutes for a runny yolk or 4 minutes for slightly firmer yolks.
Remove the eggs with a slotted spoon and set aside on paper towels to drain.
In a separate small saucepan, melt the butter over low heat, then add the heavy cream and stir until the mixture is smooth and slightly thickened.
Add the dry spinach to the cream mixture and cook just until wilted, about 1 minute.
Meanwhile, toast the pine nuts in a dry skillet over medium heat for 2–3 minutes, stirring frequently until fragrant and lightly browned.
Add the toasted pine nuts to the cream sauce, then stir in the smoked salmon and crumbled feta; heat through for 1 minute.
Divide the sauce among four bowls, top each with a poached egg, and sprinkle with smoked paprika, salt, and pepper to taste. Serve immediately.
